title: Skopeo --retry_times flag not retrying on 502 and 500 errors
body:
Hey folks, 

I have added the `retry_times` to a `skopeo copy` command but on a 502 gateway failure. I looked at the documentation and previous issues/ code examples and I think I am using the flag correctly. After running this command `skopeo copy --retry-times=3 --src-authfile <skopeo_src_auth.json> --dest-authfile <skopeo_dest_auth.json> docker://bitnamilegacy/kubectl <rehosted_images url>/bitnamilegacy/kubectl` 

I get `level=fatal msg="initializing source docker://bitnamilegacy/kubectl:latest: pinging container registry registry-1.docker.io: received unexpected HTTP status: 502 Bad Gateway"` and the command does not retry. Is this the expected behavior? I thought with the `--retry-times=3` there would be 3 retries with exponential backoff based on the documentation provided.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

That is version-dependent, compare https://github.com/containers/container-libs/issues/72 / https://github.com/containers/common/pull/2378 .
Thank you for the response, I will check the version of skopeo we use. Is it safe to assume that the latest version of skopeo will contain these fixes from the container libraries.
I did now verify that 1.22.0 is supposed to retry on 502; I didn’t check at which exact version that changed.
I will investigate and comment the version I am using to narrow the search tomorrow. Thank you again for the swift response!
The version we are using is `skopeo version 1.18.0` so somewhere between 1.22.0 and 1.18.0 retrying on 502 is turned on, I will try updating my version of skopeo thank you!
It looks like the last stable skopeo release for the apt package manager was version `1.18.0`. Is there a cadence for when this stable version gets updated for skopeo?
Is that on Debian? @siretart is authoritative there.
Yes it is on debian, more specifically it is on the jenkins/inbound-agent image which i believe uses Debian
Hey folks sorry just double checking @siretart are there any plans to update the stable Debian version of skopeo to be 1.22.0?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@michelle-tan-schrodinger Debian stable update policy only allows targeted fixes for critical issues. To get this updated, you'd need to demonstrate that this issue is critical and propose a targeted fix. We could then consider backporting the patch. I'm not currently convinced this qualifies.

Seems this issue was fixed in 1.22 or earlier. @mtrmac I think we can close this issue (I don't have permissions).

@michelle-tan-schrodinger please file a bug in Debian about backporting it, if you think there is enough convincing reasons for updating stable, see the list of criteria in https://www.debian.org/doc/manuals/developers-reference/pkgs.html#upload-stable

@siretart Ok, just to clarify, the issue was fixed in version 1.22 but I have to specifically submit a request to backport this fix from 1.22 to 1.18 for Debian. There is no plan to update the Debian stable version to 1.22.
